name: SuperAGI
tagline: Open-source framework to build, manage and run autonomous AI agents
seoTitle: SuperAGI — Build & Run Autonomous AI Agents
metaDescription: SuperAGI is an open-source, dev-first framework to build, manage and run autonomous AI agents. It ships a web GUI, reusable toolkits and concurrent agent runs.
overview: SuperAGI is an open-source, developer-first framework for building, managing and running useful autonomous AI agents. You define an agent and a goal, and the agent works through the task on its own, improving with each run.

Instead of working only from the command line, SuperAGI gives you a graphical user interface to create and watch agents, an action console to give them input and permissions, and a marketplace of toolkits that connect agents to outside systems. It can run several agents at the same time and track their performance.
features: Provision, spawn and deploy autonomous AI agents, and run several of them at the same time
Extend agent capabilities with toolkits from the marketplace that connect to external systems and third-party plugins
Graphical user interface plus an action console for giving agents input and granting permissions
Connect to multiple vector databases and store agent memory so agents can learn and adapt across runs
Performance telemetry and token-usage controls to monitor agents and manage costs
Predefined ReAct-style workflows to automate multi-step tasks

gettingStarted.intro: You can run SuperAGI in the hosted cloud or locally with Docker. The local setup below comes straight from the project README and is the usual way to self-host it.

steps.heading: Clone the repository
steps.body: Open your terminal and clone the SuperAGI repository, then move into the project folder.

steps.heading: Create your config file
steps.body: Make a copy of config_template.yaml and name it config.yaml. Make sure Docker is installed and Docker Desktop is running before the next step.

steps.heading: Start SuperAGI with Docker
steps.body: From the SuperAGI directory, build and start the stack with Docker Compose. For regular usage run the default file; if you want to use local LLMs and have a GPU, use the GPU compose file instead.
steps.code:
docker compose -f docker-compose.yaml up --build
# or, with a GPU for local LLMs:
docker compose -f docker-compose-gpu.yml up --build

steps.heading: Open the web interface
steps.body: Open your web browser and go to http://localhost:3000 to access the SuperAGI graphical interface and start creating agents.

useCases: Run multiple autonomous agents at once to work through tasks like research, content creation or data gathering
Add toolkits so agents can act in outside systems such as search, email, file management, GitHub or Jira
Give agents long-term memory with a vector database so they improve across repeated runs
Monitor agent performance and control token usage to keep costs predictable in production
